title: acer opalus subsp. neapolitanum
intro: Acer opalus subsp. neapolitanum, also known as the Italian maple, is a subspecies of the maple tree native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Acer opalus subsp. neapolitanum include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe sore throats and alleviate congestion.
